summary.intent: Reduce the verbosity of test output in the Cuebot CI/CD pipeline, which was printing full stack traces, SQL queries, and debug logs.
summary.outcome: Removed the `--info` flag from both Gradle test commands (CY2023 and CY2024 jobs) in the testing pipeline, dropping Gradle to the default lifecycle log level.

repo: The Cuebot CI pipeline has two parallel jobs — one for CY2023 and one for CY2024 — both in .github/workflows/testing-pipeline.yml
Gradle's --info flag was the root cause of verbose output: it enables Spring, Hibernate, and embedded Postgres debug/info logging including full SQL queries and stack traces
--stacktrace is a separate Gradle flag that only affects build errors, not test output verbosity — safe to keep

code.finding: Both CY2023 and CY2024 Cuebot test jobs used `--info` on Gradle commands, causing excessive CI log verbosity

workflow: Gradle log levels: lifecycle (default) shows pass/fail and assertion errors; --info adds Spring/Hibernate/SQL debug noise; --stacktrace only affects Gradle build-level errors
When CI logs are too noisy, check for Gradle --info or --debug flags before investigating application-level logging config
